houses.name: Witch's House
houses.description: Completed in 1921, it was originally a Hollywood prop created by Harry Oliver, but was relocated to Walden Drive in the 1930's.

houses.name: The Snow White Cottage
houses.description: A handcrafted masterpiece in Olalla, Washington. This home looks like it was pulled from the Disney film 'Snow White', and in fact that movie served as the inspiration for this house.

houses.name: Pink Cottage
houses.description: This house was designed by Hugh Comstock, but built by Thomas M. Browne. It's been renovated recently in the 1980's.

houses.name: Carmel Weavers Studio
houses.description: Ruth Kuster and her fellow weavers requested this Tudor style little studio form Lee Gottfried and it has been moved around Carmel and has served many purposes. It is now the Cottage of Sweets

houses.name: The Hobby House
houses.description: This was a knickknacks and furniture shop owned by Bonnie Lee, and is probably Carmel's most iconic building

houses.name: Mushroom Houses of Michigan
houses.description: Probably in a gray area, as these houses were started in the 1940's by Earl Young, but he would continue his work until his death in 1975. From there people took over and these houses were completed in the 2000's.

houses.name: Tam O'Shanter Inn
houses.description: Yet another masterpiece contributed by Harry Oliver, this restaurant in Los Angeles is an example of crooked styling achieved by movie set building techniques. This was also the favored hang out spot for Walt Disney and his Animators.

houses.name: The Muscatine Avenue Moffitt Cottage
houses.description: This is just one of many cottages built by self taught architect Howard Moffitt, this being a rare example of Storybook Style thriving in the midwest.

houses.name: The Santarella Gingerbread House
houses.description: While technically finished in 1932 during the golden age of Storybook Style, like the Mushroom Houses in Michigan, it too underwent a massive restoration effort and is recognized as a contemporary piece

houses.name: The Gingerbread House of Bay Ridge
houses.description: Built during the Arts and Crafts era for shipping mogul Howard Jones, it nonetheless has the air of fairy-tale whimsy that would characterize the Storybook movement in the coming decades.

houses.name: The Hansel and Gretel House
houses.description: The house that kicked off the trend of Storybook houses in Carmel By The Sea, this house was built by Comstock for his wife based on the dollhouses that she made for her business.

houses.name: The Whimsy House of Orcas Island
houses.description: Built by SunRay Kelley who had an evolutionary and organic outlook on architecture, this is just one of many of his Storybook architectural wonders.

houses.name: Waldo Rosebush House
houses.description: After obtaining great success with his Paper Mill

houses.name: Pod House of Perinton, New York
houses.description: You could mistake this house for a cluster of UFO's, this house completed in 1972 for an Attorney-Artist couple. They tried to sell the house for $1,100,000, but settled for $712,000

houses.name: Storybook House by the Sea
houses.description: A more recent addition, this house is only 450-500 sq. feet, but packs a lot personality into a small space!
